paper_metadata.title: Opinions of EU citizens about climate policy in their own words
paper_metadata.authors: Ivan Savin; Jeroen van den Bergh
paper_metadata.empirical_scope_or_sample: Open-ended responses from 1,266 validated respondents in Austria, Czechia, Denmark, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Italy, the Netherlands, Poland, Slovenia, Spain, and Sweden.
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: The paper analyzes open-ended survey answers about preferred climate policy measures and identifies a distinct carbon taxation topic, then relates that topic to respondent characteristics.

determinants.definition: Respondent is female rather than male.
determinants.mechanism: Women were less likely to mention carbon taxation as a preferred climate policy, indicating lower carbon-tax preference in the open-ended support responses.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Open-ended question on which climate policy measures respondents support the most; Topic 11 was carbon taxation.
determinants.outcome_variable: Prevalence of mentioning the carbon taxation topic (T11) in open-ended responses.
determinants.evidence_quote: Male respondents are more likely to support ... carbon taxation.

determinants.definition: How informed respondents say they are about Fit for 55 climate policies.
determinants.mechanism: Higher perceived knowledge is associated with lower prevalence of mentioning carbon taxation, suggesting informed respondents were less likely to select tax-based climate policy as their preferred measure.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Open-ended question on preferred climate mitigation measures; carbon taxation topic T11.
determinants.outcome_variable: Prevalence of mentioning the carbon taxation topic (T11) in open-ended responses.
determinants.evidence_quote: Perceived knowledge of climate policy ... Topic 11 Carbon taxation

exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: The narrative suggests younger people emphasize carbon taxation, but the table shows no clear significant age effect for Topic 11.
The Topic 11 coefficient on general climate-policy support is not statistically significant in Table A8.
No clear or significant association with Topic 11 in Table A8.
